prevalence.value: rare; sporadic outbreaks in limited geographies
prevalence.trend: sporadic; surveillance strengthened after reported clusters

burden.dalyPerCapita: minimal at population level; localized maternal/infant burden in outbreaks

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: RT-PCR early in illness; serology with flavivirus cross-reactivity; case definitions per national guidance

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; targeted testing during clusters and in pregnancy when indicated
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Supportive care; prevention relies on vector control and pregnancy-focused risk communication

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ICMR/NCDC-led outbreak investigations with centralized lab confirmation
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive management; pregnancy counseling and fetal monitoring during outbreaks
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited routine testing capacity outside outbreaks and differential diagnosis with dengue/chikungunya
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Rapid outbreak investigation capability through ICMR network and established vector control programmes
